description:
A team member left you two monthly sales CSV files.  
        • /home/user/data/sales_january.csv  
        • /home/user/data/sales_february.csv  

    Each file uses exactly this comma-separated, header-first schema (no extra spaces):
        Date,Region,Product,Units,Unit_Price

    Your assignment as the data analyst is to prepare a Q1 “East-region” extract and leave a tiny log for auditing.

    Required end-state of the system
    --------------------------------
    1. Create the directory /home/user/output if it does not already exist.  
    2. Produce the new file /home/user/output/east_sales_q1.csv that meets **all** of the following rules:  
       a. It contains the same header line as the source files.  
       b. It contains only the rows whose Region field is exactly the case-sensitive string East.  
       c. All qualifying rows from **both** source files must be included.  
       d. The rows must be ordered by the Date column in ascending, ISO-8601 order (yyyy-mm-dd).  
       e. Fields must stay comma-separated; no leading or trailing spaces may be added.  

       The correct final contents of /home/user/output/east_sales_q1.csv must therefore be **exactly**:

            Date,Region,Product,Units,Unit_Price
            2021-01-03,East,Gadget,10,19.99
            2021-01-10,East,Widget,7,25.00
            2021-02-15,East,Widget,9,25.00
            2021-02-20,East,Gadget,4,19.99

       (A newline character must follow every line, including the last one.)

    3. Create a plain-text audit file /home/user/output/process.log with **exactly** two lines and no extra blank lines:  
            Rows_selected: 4
            Source_files: sales_january.csv,sales_february.csv
       The spelling, colons, commas and capitalization must match precisely; each line must end with a single newline.

    Hints for your shell workflow (do **not** list these commands verbatim in the final solution):  
      • You will probably want to use tools such as grep, awk, sort, head or tail, and redirection (>, >>).  
      • Remember to retain the header only once.  
      • Make sure /home/user/output exists before you write files there.

    When everything is done, you can cat the two files to visually confirm they match the specifications.
truth:
Initial data files that are present before the task starts:

    Path: /home/user/data/sales_january.csv  
    Contents (each line must end with a newline):
        Date,Region,Product,Units,Unit_Price
        2021-01-03,East,Gadget,10,19.99
        2021-01-05,West,Widget,5,25.00
        2021-01-10,East,Widget,7,25.00

    Path: /home/user/data/sales_february.csv  
    Contents:
        Date,Region,Product,Units,Unit_Price
        2021-02-12,North,Gadget,3,19.99
        2021-02-15,East,Widget,9,25.00
        2021-02-20,East,Gadget,4,19.99

    Verification data after the agent finishes:

    1. /home/user/output/east_sales_q1.csv must exist and be byte-exactly:
        Date,Region,Product,Units,Unit_Price\n
        2021-01-03,East,Gadget,10,19.99\n
        2021-01-10,East,Widget,7,25.00\n
        2021-02-15,East,Widget,9,25.00\n
        2021-02-20,East,Gadget,4,19.99\n

    2. /home/user/output/process.log must exist and be byte-exactly:
        Rows_selected: 4\n
        Source_files: sales_january.csv,sales_february.csv\n
